confirmed.title: Receipt-side Tier-2 (aux_zero_fallback) skips the Wave-5B dedup that the demand side applies — every BOM-aux sibling claims the full aux=0 receipt/pick total
confirmed.field_or_branch: _get Tier 2 branch — prod_receipt_real_qty / pick_actual_qty / purchase_* / subcontract_* / delivery_real_qty on Step-1 BOM rows
confirmed.paths_affected: default live path (Step 1 _make_row rows); cache ?source=cache has its own SQL CASE (not compared, low value)
confirmed.claim: When one material code has N>1 BOM aux groups (all aux≠0) and receipts/picks are recorded at aux=0 (the canonical aux_zero_fallback shape), EVERY sibling row displays the full aux-0 total, so SUM(入库/领料) by code = N × Kingdee. Meanwhile demand (need_qty) for the same shape IS deduped (elected sibling gets the remainder, others 0 via _tier_2_5_state at 1175-1182), so sibling rows show e.g. 订单数量 0 but 领料/入库 = full total, and code-level fulfillment >100%.
confirmed.impact: On the default live MTO query, any material code with 2+ BOM aux groups (e.g., color-variant 贴纸/镜片) whose receipts/picks were recorded at aux=0 shows the FULL aux-0 total (入库/领料/采购入库/委外入库/销售出库) on EVERY sibling row — code-level sum = N × live Kingdee. For self-made codes the demand side IS deduped, so non-elected sibling rows display 订单数量 0 alongside full 入库/领料, yielding >100%/nonsense per-row fulfillment.
confirmed.verifier_reasoning: Re-derived from the code, not the scanner's evidence. (1) mto_handler.py:836-866 `_get`: the Tier-2 branch (aux!=0, `_by_code` hit) returns at line 843 BEFORE consulting `_recv_tier_state` — the dedup state is only consulted in Tier 2.5 (845-849) and Tier 3 (858-862). The state for this exact shape IS built at 1117-1140 (exact_matched_aux empty when receipts only at (c,0)) but is dead code for Tier 2. So with N>1 BOM aux≠0 sibling groups and receipts at aux=0, every sibling returns the full aux-0 total → SUM by code = N × Kingdee. (2) The demand side IS deduped for the symmetric shape: Step 1 lines 1175-1182 consult _tier_2_5_state before _lookup_mo_qty, zeroing non-elected siblings — so a sibling row can show 订单数量=0 with 领料/入库=full. (3) Not by-design: the cache mirror `_apply_recv_partial_match_dedup` (cache_reader.py:889-929) treats ANY non-'exact' quality — including 'aux_zero_fallback' — as non_matched and zeroes non-elected siblings, i.e. the cache path dedups Tier 2 while the live path does not, a live/cache divergence in code that explicitly claims to mirror (cache_reader.py:844-846). The Wave-5B comment (mto_handler.py:785-793) names 'SUM by code = N × actual receipt total' as the bug class being killed; Tier 2 produces that shape and was left out. (4) No deliberate pin: the only Tier-2 live test (test_mto_handler.py:3678-3696) uses a single BOM-aux group. Reachable on the default path: Step 1 _make_row is the live builder used by _fetch_live. Distinct from the excluded is_purchase bug and not on the known-by-design list. Severity high: wrong 入库/领料/采购/委外/出库 numbers on the default live path for any code with multiple aux variants whose receipts were entered at aux=0 (the canonical aux_zero_fallback shape, common enough to have dedicated telemetry); mitigated only by the generic ⚠ fallback badge, which flags fallback use, not N×-counting.

confirmed.title: Synthetic multi-aux rows (2b/2c) bypass _recv_tier_state entirely — dedup state is keyed only off BOM codes, so each variant claims the full pick/receipt rollup
confirmed.lines: 1060-1062 (_bom_codes_seen built from bom_groups only), 845-855 (no-state fallthrough to full _by_code_all), 1282-1296 (2b multi-aux by design), 1316-1342 (2c multi-aux by Wave-6C design)
confirmed.field_or_branch: pick_actual_qty / pick_app_qty / purchase_receipt_real_qty / delivery_real_qty on synthetic blocks 2b and 2c
confirmed.paths_affected: default live path (synthetic rows only — materials not in PPBOM)
confirmed.claim: Blocks 2b (Bug-5b: purchased multi-aux SKUs, e.g. 03.23.009 贴纸 with 3 color variants) and 2c (Wave-6C: multi-aux PRD_MO variants, e.g. DK251003S 05.20.01.07.011 with 3 aux) deliberately emit one row PER aux variant. For each such row, _get on picks/receipts recorded at aux=0 or disjoint aux finds NO _recv_tier_state entry (state is built only over `_bom_codes_seen`, which is seeded exclusively from bom_groups at 1060-1062) and falls through to the full `_by_code` (Tier 2) or `_by_code_all` (845-855 "No state ⇒ ... return the full rollup") — so all N variant rows each display the full 领料数量 total → code-level SUM = N × Kingdee in the 领料 column (and purchase_receipt/delivery fields, where surfaced).
confirmed.impact: For materials not in PPBOM that have multiple aux variants (emitted as N rows by synthetic blocks 2b/2c on the default live path), receipt-side columns (领料数量, 采购实收, 生产入库, 销售出库 etc.) can each show the FULL cross-aux rollup on every variant row whenever the receipt/pick aux keys don't exactly match the row aux — so the visible per-code total is N× the real Kingdee total. Guaranteed double-count sub-case: a synthetic code with both an aux=0 row and specific-aux rows (aux=0 row claims the full Tier-3 rollup while specific rows claim exact amounts). Example shapes: 2c rows like DK251003S/05.20.01.07.011 (3 PRD_MO aux, no PPBOM/PRD_INSTOCK) with picks at disjoint aux; 2b purchased multi-aux SKUs like 03.23.009 with receipts at aux=0 or disjoint aux.
confirmed.verifier_reasoning: Confirmed by re-reading the code. (1) _bom_codes_seen is seeded only from bom_groups.keys() (mto_handler.py:1060-1062) and _recv_tier_state is built only over those codes (1117-1140), so synthetic codes never get Wave-5B dedup state. (2) _get's no-state fallthrough (845-855 for aux≠0 Tier 2.5; 856-865 for aux=0 Tier 3) returns the full _by_code_all rollup; the comment's safety claim ('single BOM-aux group ... no double count') assumes one row per code, which blocks 2b (1282-1296, explicitly multi-row per Bug-5b comment 1230-1234) and 2c Wave-6C carve-out (1316-1342) deliberately violate. (3) Every synthetic row goes through _make_row (898-934) which calls _get for all 10 receipt lookups. (4) Reachable on the default path: _fetch_live (571) calls _build_bom_joined_rows_from_live at line 683. (5) Not in the by-design list and distinct from the known is_purchase bug. Severity stays medium, not high: it needs a data conjunction (synthetic multi-aux code AND receipt aux not exactly matching row aux); both halves are documented real shapes in this tenant (Bug-5b/Wave-6C multi-aux synthetics; Wave-5B disjoint aux), but no confirmed live instance of the conjunction was demonstrated, and affected rows carry the all_aux_rollup match-quality badge so the inflation is at least flagged. Confidence: high on mechanics, medium on real-world frequency.

confirmed.title: Wave-5B/strict-aux deliberate zeroing of sibling purchase/subcontract order qty is resurrected by the `or need_qty` B1 fallback — Decimal('0') consumed as 'no order exists'
confirmed.field_or_branch: purchase_order_qty=row.purchase_order_qty or row.need_qty (外购 branch); purchase_order_qty=row.subcontract_order_qty or row.need_qty (委外 branch)
confirmed.paths_affected: default live path (purchased/subcontracted materials with multiple BOM-aux groups + PO/委外单 at disjoint aux); also ?strict_aux=true (power-user)
confirmed.claim: The B1 fix (1db7b79) made demand fall back to need_qty 'when no order exists'. But row.purchase_order_qty == Decimal('0') has TWO meanings: (a) genuinely no PO, (b) a real PO exists but Wave-5B dedup attributed it to the elected sibling and intentionally zeroed this row (_get returns ZERO at 849/862 for non-elected aux). The `or` at 1743/1761 can't distinguish them, so a dedup-zeroed sibling row displays its BOM need_qty as 订单数量 — code-level 订单数量 SUM = full PO total (on the elected row) + need_qty fragments (on siblings), inflating demand and breaking the dedup invariant the Wave-5B code just enforced. Same resurrection happens after _apply_strict_aux_filter explicitly writes purchase_order_qty=ZERO (250-259): the user asked for non-exact data to be zeroed, and the branch fallback paints need_qty back in.
confirmed.impact: For a purchased or subcontracted material appearing in PPBOM under 2+ aux groups (e.g., color-variant 03.xx carton/纸卡) whose PO/委外单 is recorded at aux IDs disjoint from the BOM aux: the elected BOM-aux row correctly shows the full PO total as 订单数量, but each non-elected sibling row — which Wave-5B deliberately zeroed so SUM matches Kingdee — displays its BOM need_qty as 订单数量 instead of 0. Code-level SUM(订单数量) = PO total + need_qty fragments, inflated vs the actual Kingdee PO, and the sibling rows show 0% 完成率 against a phantom demand. Under ?strict_aux=true the effect is deterministic and broader: every 外购/委外 row whose PO matched via aux_zero_fallback/all_aux_rollup has its filter-zeroed 订单数量 silently repainted with need_qty, defeating the strict filter's purpose of making non-exact data visible.
confirmed.verifier_reasoning: Attempted refutation failed; every cited mechanism re-verified in src/query/mto_handler.py. (1) Lines 1743/1761 use `row.purchase_order_qty or row.need_qty` / `row.subcontract_order_qty or row.need_qty`; the comment (1738-1742) only contemplates 'no PO yet'. (2) Wave-5B dedup state `_recv_tier_state` is built for labels `po_order` (line 1110) and `sub_order` (line 1113); `_get` returns ZERO for non-elected non-matched aux (lines 847-849, 860-862); `_make_row` feeds purchase_order_qty/subcontract_order_qty through `_get` (lines 914, 917). So a deliberate dedup zero (real PO attributed to elected sibling) is byte-identical to 'no PO' when consumed by the `or` — classic Pattern 7. (3) No discriminator survives: match_quality_breakdown intentionally reports the would-have-applied tier, not the zeroing (docstring lines 874-878), so the resurrected need_qty even carries a misleading 'all_aux_rollup' provenance. (4) Strict-aux interplay confirmed: _apply_strict_aux_filter zeroes purchase_order_qty/subcontract_order_qty (lines 240/242) at line 691 BEFORE _bom_row_to_child at 694, so the `or` deterministically repaints need_qty over the filter's deliberate zeros for any non-exact PO match. (5) Default-path reachable: get_status routes everything to _fetch_live (line 216); Step 1 BOM rows → _make_row → _bom_row_to_child branch effective_type==2 at 1728 (distinct from the known is_purchase/外销包材 bug at 1682). Not in the by-design list and contradicts Wave-5B's stated SUM==Kingdee invariant (comments 790-808). Severity medium is correct: the default-path trigger requires a multi-aux-group purchased/subcontracted BOM code with PO/委外单 at disjoint aux (a data family Wave-5B documents as real — AK2510034/05.02.15.62, AS2602033 — though cited cases are receipt-side, not PO-side), and the deterministic variant is on the power-user ?strict_aux path.

confirmed.lines: 616-617 (aux collection omits prod_orders), 1336 (2c passes getattr(first,'aux_attributes','')), 1628 (both fallback legs empty); supporting: src/readers/factory.py:278-279
confirmed.field_or_branch: aux_attributes / aux_descriptions for synthetic block 2c rows
confirmed.paths_affected: default live path — self-made materials with PRD_MO but no PPBOM entry and no PRD_INSTOCK yet (early-stage production), especially Wave-6C multi-aux variants

Worst case is the Wave-6C multi-aux carve-out the code itself documents (e.g. DK251003S 05.20.01.07.011 with 3 PRD_MO aux variants): the dashboard shows N rows with identical material code/name/spec and an empty aux column, so the color/size variants are indistinguishable except by quantity.
confirmed.verifier_reasoning: Verified every cited line by direct read. (1) mto_handler.py:616-617 collects aux_prop_ids from [sales_orders, purchase_orders, prod_receipts, sales_deliveries, material_picks, production_bom] — prod_orders omitted, so PRD_MO-only aux ids never enter the line-623 lookup_aux_properties call. (2) Block 2c (1316-1342) emits synthetic rows from prod_orders with aux_attributes=getattr(first,'aux_attributes','') where ProductionOrderModel.aux_attributes defaults to '' (models.py:25) and factory.py PRD_MO config (271-289) has no aux_attributes mapping (comment at 278 confirms FAuxPropId.FName doesn't exist on PRD_MO). (3) _bom_row_to_child:1628 computes aux_descriptions.get(row.aux_prop_id,'') or row.aux_attributes — both legs empty for these rows. (4) Reachable on default path: _fetch_live (571) calls _build_bom_joined_rows_from_live at 683 and _bom_row_to_child at 694 with the deficient aux_descriptions dict. (5) Frontend renders aux_attributes as a default-visible column (dashboard.js:33, dashboard.html:453-455). Not in the known-by-design list and distinct from the known is_purchase bug. 2d is NOT affected (material_picks is in the collection list), so the gap is correctly scoped to 2c. confirmed.title: B1 `or row.need_qty` fallback resurrects deliberate dedup/strict-aux zeros on 采购/委外 订单数量 — Decimal('0') consumed as 'no order exists'
confirmed.field_or_branch: BOMJoinedRow.purchase_order_qty / subcontract_order_qty in _bom_row_to_child branches effective_type==2 (外购/包材) and effective_type==3 (委外)
confirmed.paths_affected: Live DEFAULT path (_fetch_live -> _build_bom_joined_rows_from_live -> _bom_row_to_child), for purchased/外销包材/委外 codes with >=2 PPBOM aux groups whose PUR_PurchaseOrder/SUB_POORDER rows sit at disjoint aux numbering (the exact AS2602033/AK2510034-style shape Wave 5B was built for); also ?strict_aux=true on any non-exact-matched row; also any genuine FQty=0 order line
confirmed.claim: 1) Dedup case: Wave-5B `_recv_tier_state` covers po_order and sub_order (lines 1110, 1113). When a purchased code has 2+ BOM aux groups and the PO is at disjoint aux, `_get` deliberately returns ZERO for every non-elected sibling (lines 847-849, 860-862) so SUM(订单数量) == Kingdee PO total. The commit-1db7b79 fallback `purchase_order_qty=row.purchase_order_qty or row.need_qty` (1743; same for 委外 at 1761) cannot distinguish that deliberate Decimal('0') from 'no PO exists', so each non-elected sibling row now displays 订单数量 = its BOM need_qty. User-visible wrong output: SUM(订单数量) for the code = real PO total + Σ need_qty(siblings) — over-counts vs Kingdee 采购订单; the sibling's demand_qty metric and fulfillment_rate (config/mto_config.json:111 demand_field=purchase_order_qty) are computed against the resurrected need_qty instead of 0, and its match_quality badge still says all_aux_rollup while the displayed number is actually BOM-sourced. Same applies when remainder==0 (PO fully claimed by exact-matched siblings): the elected row also gets ZERO and is resurrected to need_qty. This is a regression interaction: before 1db7b79 these siblings correctly showed 0. 2) strict_aux case: _apply_strict_aux_filter (253-256) explicitly zeroes purchase_order_qty/subcontract_order_qty for non-exact rows, then 1743/1761 resurrects them to need_qty — the power-user 'no fallbacks' mode silently re-introduces a fallback value as 订单数量. 3) Minor: a legitimate zero-qty order line (FQty=0) is likewise indistinguishable from no-PO and displays need_qty.
confirmed.impact: For a 委外加工 material with 2+ BOM aux groups whose 委外订单 rows sit at disjoint aux numbering (the documented AK2510034/AS2602033-style shape), each non-elected sibling row shows 订单数量 = its BOM need_qty instead of the deliberate 0, so the per-code SUM of 订单数量 exceeds the real Kingdee 委外订单 total and fulfillment rates are computed against the inflated denominator; the ⚠ match-quality badge still claims the order data came from an aux rollup. Same mechanism hits 外购 packaging via line 1743 once the known is_purchase bug is fixed (today that branch is mostly shadowed by it). In ?strict_aux=true mode, the explicitly zeroed order quantities are silently resurrected to BOM need_qty, defeating the no-fallback contract. A genuine FQty=0 order line also displays need_qty instead of 0.
confirmed.verifier_reasoning: Re-derived every cited line. Consumer: mto_handler.py:1743 `row.purchase_order_qty or row.need_qty` and :1761 `row.subcontract_order_qty or row.need_qty` — `or` on Decimal makes Decimal('0') indistinguishable from 'no order'. Producer 1 (deliberate zeros, default live path): _get (845-849, 858-862) returns ZERO for non-elected sibling aux groups when _recv_tier_state has an entry; _recv_lookups (1105-1116) includes po_order and sub_order; state built over _bom_codes_seen (1060-1062, all BOM codes, no type filter) at 1117-1140 whenever order rollup>0 and some BOM aux lacks an exact match; remainder==0 case (1130) zeroes even the elected row. _make_row wires _get into purchase_order_qty (914) / subcontract_order_qty (917). So Wave 5B's deliberate dedup zeros (designed so SUM(订单数量)==Kingdee order total) are resurrected to BOM need_qty (for non-self-made rows: SUM of that group's PPBOM lines, line 1197) → SUM over-counts vs Kingdee, and demand/fulfillment_rate use the resurrected value while the match_quality badge still reports the rollup tier (per _get_quality docstring 874-878, intentionally tier-not-zero-aware). Producer 2: _apply_strict_aux_filter (237-259) explicitly zeroes purchase_order_qty/subcontract_order_qty for non-exact rows; 1743/1761 then resurrect them, contradicting the strict-mode contract stated in its own docstring (226-235). The fallback comments (1738-1742, 1759-1760) only contemplate 'no PO yet' — neither producer is considered; not by-design, not on the known-by-design list. Reachability correction: the scanner overstated the 外购/包材 branch — on TODAY'S live path the effective_type==2 branch (1728/1743) is nearly dead because the KNOWN is_purchase=False bug routes 外销包材 to the SELF_MADE branch (1682) and 主料/辅料 map to type 1 (1607-1613); only unmapped-category + material_type==2 fallback rows (~0.06%, PPBOM FMaterialType is flat '1') reach it. BUT the 委外 branch (1761) IS default-path reachable: category_name flows live (factory.py:315 FMaterialId.FCategoryId → _make_row call at 1211) and 委外加工 maps unconditionally to effective_type==3. Concrete scenario: a 委外加工 code with 2+ PPBOM aux (color) groups whose SUB_POORDER rows sit at disjoint aux numbering — the exact AK2510034/AS2602033 shape documented at 1099-1104 as the Wave 5B motivation — shows 订单数量=need_qty on every non-elected sibling instead of 0. The 1743 branch additionally becomes hot the moment the planned is_purchase fix lands, inheriting the same regression. Severity stays medium: default-path wrong numbers, but conditional on the multi-aux-disjoint data shape; strict_aux and FQty=0 sub-cases are edge-path. Pattern-7 falsy trap (deliberate Decimal('0') consumed as 'missing') introduced by 1db7b79 on top of the Wave 5B invariant.

confirmed.verifier_reasoning: I attempted to refute and could not — every link in the chain checks out on re-read. (1) Producer: mto_handler.py:1446-1449 looks up STK_InStock receipts into purchase_stock_in_qty, and the ChildItem is built with material_type=1 / is_finished_goods=True / purchase_stock_in_qty=... ChildItem serializes material_type under alias material_type_code, so item.material_type_code===1 fails every gate. (4) Metrics: enrichment.py:32-40 maps (material_type=1, is_finished_goods=True) to finished_goods; config/mto_config.json:22-23 sets fulfilled_field=prod_instock_real_qty only; mto_config.py:272 registers it; metrics.py:117+155-179 computes rate from that single field → fulfilled=0, demand=sales_order_qty>0 → 0%/not_started. confirmed.paths_affected: DEFAULT path: /api/alerts/over-ship (alerts.py:91 → get_over_ship_alerts cache_reader.py:1300-1333 — numerator SUM(real_qty) from cached_sales_delivery, denominator SUM(qty) from cached_sales_orders); also 07.xx delivered/order qty on ?source=cache (mto_handler.py:433,450-453)
confirmed.claim: Shipped qty is under-counted (false negatives: real 超发 hidden) and order qty is under-counted (false positives: phantom 超发). Live example: delivery XS26050001 / 2603089单 / 03.11-family 03.03.001 aux=196059 has 4 lines 186+55+54+186=481 — cache keeps only the last (186), -61%. Sales order XSDD2605036 / DS264105S / 07.32.001 aux=227785 has lines 300 and 3 — the keep-FIRST dedup stores 300 and silently drops the 3 (the docstring at sync_service.py:953 claims 'keeping the record with MAX qty' but the code at 971-972 keeps first — doc/code drift). With both sides wrong by different amounts, individual alert rows and total_count are unreliable.
confirmed.impact: On the deployed 预警 (alerts) page, 超发 (over-ship) rows show wrong shipped and order quantities in both directions: real over-shipments are hidden (delivery keep-last collapse under-counts shipped qty, e.g. XS26050001 stores 186 of a true 481 for 03.03.001 — a −61% under-count that can suppress a genuine alert) and phantom over-ship alerts can appear (sales-order keep-first collapse under-counts the order denominator, e.g. XSDD2605036/07.32.001 drops a 3-unit line). At ~192-228 affected document/material groups per month live, individual alert rows, over_amount values, and total_count are all unreliable. Secondary: 07.xx delivered/order qty on ?source=cache MTO queries (low value, path slated for deletion).
confirmed.verifier_reasoning: Attempted refutation failed on every axis. (1) Code re-read confirms: sync_service.py:917-921 delivery dedup keeps LAST record per (bill_no,mto,material,aux) via dict overwrite; sync_service.py:959-973 sales-order dedup keeps FIRST while the docstring at line 953 falsely claims 'keeping the record with MAX qty' (doc/code drift); the warning at 966-970 fires then silently drops the line. schema.sql:175,199 UNIQUE constraints lack entry-line grain, and factory.py SALES_DELIVERY_CONFIG/SALES_ORDER_CONFIG fetch entry-level fields without FEntryID/FSeq, so distinct same-document lines are indistinguishable and collapse. (2) I independently re-derived the empirical claim with my own live Kingdee probe (May-2026, FDocumentStatus B/C/D): SAL_OUTSTOCK 235 duplicate (bill,mto,material,aux) groups in 7,838 rows (192 with differing qtys, ~54.7K units lost/month under keep-last); SAL_SaleOrder 253 dup groups in 4,536 rows (228 differing). Both named examples reproduced EXACTLY: XS26050001/2603089单/03.03.001 aux=196059 lines [186,55,54,186]=481 true vs 186 stored (−61%); XSDD2605036/DS264105S/07.32.001 aux=227785 lines [300,3] → 300 stored, 3 dropped. Scanner's exact counts (290/354) didn't reproduce under my filter (235/253), likely a different window — substance unaffected. (3) Not by-design: not on the known-by-design list, and the docstring misdescribes the actual behavior. (4) Reachability: /api/alerts/over-ship (alerts.py:73-92) is cache-backed by design with NO live alternative and powers the 预警 tab deployed to prod — default-path for that feature, not gated behind ?source=cache. cache_reader.get_over_ship_alerts (1281-1294, 1311-1329) SUMs the already-collapsed rows on both numerator and denominator. The only weak sub-claim is the MTO-dashboard 07.xx delivered/order qty impact, which is ?source=cache-only (default MTO path reads these forms live). This is the 6th occurrence of bug-patterns Pattern 5 — migration 018 fixed cached_material_picking's key but left both sales tables with the same collapse.

confirmed.verifier_reasoning: Confirmed by re-reading the code, despite the adversarial stance. (1) The collapse is unconditional: sync_service.py:717-721 (purchase orders), 803-806 (production receipts), 839-842 (purchase receipts) build dicts keyed (bill_no, mto, material, aux[, bill_type]) with last-write-wins; schema.sql:80/119/137 UNIQUE constraints make multi-line-same-material-same-document rows unrepresentable, and bill_type_number cannot help since one document has one bill type. Quantities are replaced, never summed. (2) Reachability: mto_handler.py:180-216 routes default get_status to _fetch_live (live Kingdee, sums all entry rows via _sum_by_material_and_aux); the cached consumers (get_mto_bom_joined cache_reader.py:655-752, get_purchase_orders/receipts at 188-247 called from _try_cache lines 406-438) are gated behind source=='cache'. Alerts use cached_purchase_orders only for MAX(material_name) (cache_reader.py:1214-1221), not quantities. (3) Not by-design: no comment blesses the collapse; the sibling picking table got an explicit Pattern-5 fix for the same family. (4) The scanner's specific live-probe numbers were not re-verified against Kingdee, but the mechanism does not depend on them; multi-line receipts per document are routine. One correction to the scanner: it claimed 'no live-path or alerts consumer' — true for those, but the agent-chat SQL pipeline does consume these tables for quantity sums, a real user-visible surface. Severity stays low per the audit brief: the table feeding ?source=cache is slated for deletion and the default path is clean; the agent-chat exposure is real but secondary and the agent is an analysis aid, not the numbers-of-record page.

confirmed.title: Stale-document leak: per-MTO DELETE list is derived from FETCHED records, and production orders have no DELETE at all — deleted/un-audited Kingdee documents linger in alert math forever
confirmed.lines: 484-545 (production orders: upsert only, no DELETE step) and 722, 771, 808, 844, 885, 922, 975 (mto_numbers built from records_list, not from the queried MTO set)
confirmed.field_or_branch: `mto_numbers = sorted({r.mto_number for r in records_list if r.mto_number})` → `DELETE ... WHERE mto_number IN (...)`
confirmed.paths_affected: DEFAULT path: over-pick/over-ship alerts and table_freshness row counts (whole-table scans over cached_material_picking / cached_sales_delivery / cached_sales_orders); ?source=cache lookups; only force_full (_clear_cache, line 131-132) ever purges — routine 4×/day auto-syncs never do
confirmed.claim: When the LAST document of a type for an MTO is deleted or reverted to draft in Kingdee (status A is excluded by every reader's extra_filter, factory.py:344-436), the next sync fetches zero rows for that MTO, so the MTO never enters the DELETE list and the dead rows persist indefinitely — e.g. a 反审核'd 领料单 keeps generating a 超领 alert that no longer exists in Kingdee. cached_production_orders is worse: there is no DELETE-by-MTO step at all (contrast _upsert_purchase_orders_no_commit:723-728), so cancelled production orders permanently inflate the prd_mo_agg need_qty override on the cache path.
confirmed.impact: Phantom 超领/超发 alerts on the deployed 预警 tab (and skewed over/under/net summary totals): when the only picking/delivery document of an MTO is deleted or 反审核'd (status A) in Kingdee, the next sync fetches zero rows for that MTO, the per-MTO DELETE never fires, and the dead cached row keeps generating an alert indefinitely (until force_full or a new doc on the same MTO). cached_production_orders is worse — no DELETE step at all, so deleted production orders accumulate forever — but its consumer (need_qty override) is ?source=cache-only. The default MTO query (get_status -> _fetch_live) is unaffected since it hits Kingdee live.
confirmed.verifier_reasoning: Adversarially re-derived every cited mechanic and all hold: (1) sync_service.py:722/771/808/885/922/975 build the DELETE set from fetched records ('mto_numbers = sorted({r.mto_number for r in records_list if r.mto_number})' then 'DELETE ... WHERE mto_number IN (...)') — an MTO with zero fetched rows of a type is never purged; (2) _upsert_production_orders (484-545) is INSERT...ON CONFLICT only, no DELETE — contrast _upsert_purchase_orders_no_commit:723-728; (3) factory.py:418 (PRD_PickMtrl) and :436 (SAL_OUTSTOCK) filter FDocumentStatus IN ('B','C','D'), so a draft-reverted doc is invisible to the next fetch — exactly the trigger; (4) _clear_cache only runs under force_full (run_sync:131-132) and the scheduler's routine syncs (scheduler.py:59-66, days_back=90 from sync_config.json) never force-full; (5) the consumer get_over_pick_alerts (cache_reader.py:1112-1221) / get_over_ship_alerts (:1252) do whole-table GROUP BY scans over cached_material_picking / cached_sales_delivery, backing the deployed, default-reachable /api/alerts endpoints (no ?source=cache required). Not in the known-by-design list and distinct from the known is_purchase bug. Severity stays medium, not high: the default MTO live query is unaffected; the leak needs a specific Kingdee event shape (deletion/un-audit of the LAST doc of its type for an MTO — if any sibling doc survives, the per-MTO DELETE+reinsert purges the dead row); and the production-order half's quantitative consumer is the deprecated ?source=cache path. Matches the rubric's 'wrong edge-path numbers'. Confidence high — this is bug-family Pattern 1 (tombstone-less incremental sync) with the delete-set derivation as the gap.

confirmed.title: SUB_SUBREQORDER (委外订单) reader has NO FDocumentStatus filter — draft 委外订单 counted as 订单数量
confirmed.field_or_branch: SUBCONTRACTING_ORDER_CONFIG.extra_filter (absent) → subcontract_order_qty/subcontract_stock_in_qty → _bom_row_to_child 委外 branch
confirmed.paths_affected: Default live path (_fetch_live mto_handler.py:602 → sub_order/sub_stock_in :749-750 → _make_row :917-918 → 委外 branch :1761-1762) AND cache/sync (sync uses same ReaderConfig; fetch_by_date_range applies config.extra_filter, which is empty here)
confirmed.claim: A draft (状态A 创建/未审核) 委外订单's FQty is displayed as 订单数量 for any 委外-routed material, and because the 委外 branch is `row.subcontract_order_qty or row.need_qty` (mto_handler.py:1761) a nonzero draft qty also SUPPRESSES the need_qty fallback — so the user sees an unapproved order's quantity instead of BOM demand. Inconsistent with the sibling PUR_PurchaseOrder reader feeding the SAME UI column, which explicitly excludes drafts.
confirmed.impact: On the default live MTO query, any material categorized 委外加工 (e.g., an outsourced silicone cap) shows a draft/unapproved (FDocumentStatus='A') 委外订单's FQty as 订单数量, and because of `row.subcontract_order_qty or row.need_qty` (mto_handler.py:1761) the draft quantity also overrides the BOM-demand fallback — so the dashboard displays an unapproved order's qty instead of BOM demand, and the fulfillment rate is computed against it (understating completion while the draft is larger, or inflating demand if the draft is later edited/discarded). 入库 numbers are unaffected (drafts can have no receipts).
confirmed.verifier_reasoning: Re-derived every link in the chain. (1) src/readers/factory.py:385-400 SUBCONTRACTING_ORDER_CONFIG genuinely has no extra_filter — verified by grep: only 5 extra_filter assignments exist (:344,:363,:382,:418,:436), all the other quantity-bearing readers, all "FDocumentStatus IN ('B','C','D')"; PUR_PurchaseOrder's comment (:362) explicitly says drafts are excluded for the SAME UI semantics. fetch_by_mto (factory.py:142-151) builds only `FMtoNo LIKE '...%'` when config.extra_filter is empty, so drafts (status A) come back from Kingdee. (2) Default live path: mto_handler.py:602 fetch_by_mto in _fetch_live's asyncio.gather → sums at :749-750 → _make_row :917-918 → BOMJoinedRow.subcontract_order_qty → _CATEGORY_TO_TYPE :1612 maps 委外加工→(3,委外) → branch :1749-1766 sets purchase_order_qty=row.subcontract_order_qty or row.need_qty (:1761). Fully reachable on get_status→_fetch_live. (3) Not by-design: the config has no comment at all (every sibling has one), it is not on the known-by-design list, and MEMORY.md + agent KB state the B/C/D filter as the project-wide rule. (4) Sync path equally affected (sync_service.py:392 uses the same reader via fetch_by_date_range, which only appends the empty config.extra_filter). Severity stays medium: wrong 订单数量 + fulfillment-rate on the default path, but only for 委外加工-routed materials while a draft 委外订单 exists (transient data shape), and the draft's FQty usually equals the eventually-approved value; the receipt side is unaffected since a draft order has FStockInQty=0. The scanner's claim that a nonzero draft qty suppresses the need_qty fallback via the `or` at :1761 is exactly what the code says.

confirmed.title: Documented bill-type 口径 not enforced: PRD_INSTOCK has no FBillType='SCRKD02_SYS' filter; STK_InStock sums ALL bill types, not just RKD01/RKD03
confirmed.field_or_branch: PRODUCTION_RECEIPT_CONFIG / PURCHASE_RECEIPT_CONFIG extra_filter; pur_real & receipt_real aggregation
confirmed.paths_affected: Default live path and cache (sync writes whatever the readers fetch; cache_reader/sync never filter bill_type_number either — confirmed by grep: only the UNIQUE key in sync_service.py:841-864 references it)
confirmed.claim: CLAUDE.md's data-flow spec says PRD_INSTOCK receipts = FBillType.FNumber='SCRKD02_SYS' and STK_InStock = RKD01_SYS/RKD03_SYS, but neither filter exists in code: PRD_INSTOCK's only filter is FDocumentStatus (factory.py:344) and STK_InStock fetches every bill type (bill_type_number is mapped at :378 but never filtered anywhere in src/). If this tenant ever books an MTO-tagged STK_InStock row under another type (e.g. RKD02/RKD04/RKD05) or a non-SCRKD02 PRD_INSTOCK row, it silently inflates 入库数量 for that material on the default path.
confirmed.impact: None demonstrated today. A user would only see inflated numbers if this Kingdee tenant books an MTO-tagged receipt under an undocumented bill type: e.g. a hypothetical 费用物料/受托 STK_InStock row tagged with the MTO would inflate a finished good's 采购入库 column (07.xx path), or a non-SCRKD02 PRD_INSTOCK row would inflate a 自制 item's 生产入库实收. For 外购/委外 BOM children — the scenario the scanner implies — displayed 入库 comes from PO/委外订单-side fields and is unaffected by the missing STK_InStock bill-type filter.
confirmed.verifier_reasoning: Code evidence verified exactly as claimed: factory.py:344 (PRD_INSTOCK) and :382 (STK_InStock) filter only FDocumentStatus; bill_type_number is mapped at factory.py:378 but never filtered anywhere in src/ (grep confirms only sync UNIQUE key, cache pass-through, and agent-prompt text reference it); mto_handler.py:748 sums the unsplit purchase_receipts list. So the documented SCRKD02/RKD01/RKD03 口径 is genuinely unenforced and nothing marks this as by-design. HOWEVER, the scanner overstates impact: tracing _bom_row_to_child (mto_handler.py:1616-1783) shows purchase_receipt_real_qty is consumed by NO routing branch — 外购 displays PUR_PurchaseOrder.FStockInQty (line 1744) and 委外 displays SUB_SUBREQORDER figures (line 1762), both untouched by STK_InStock bill types. The unfiltered STK sum reaches users only via the finished-goods (07.xx) purchase_stock_in_qty (mto_handler.py:630-633 + 1446) and the match-quality badge; the unfiltered PRD_INSTOCK sum feeds 自制/自制包材 生产入库实收. Impact is therefore conditional on hypothetical data (an MTO-tagged STK_InStock row of a type other than RKD01/RKD03, or a non-SCRKD02 PRD_INSTOCK row) with zero observed instances, and the repo's post-2026-04-20 doctrine (union-over-filter, Wave 6B comment mto_handler.py:1413-1419) means adding the documented filters could reintroduce the dropped-receipt bug family. Real latent doc/code divergence; severity stays low; likely correct fix is updating the doc 口径, not adding filters.

confirmed.title: SAL_SaleOrder / PRD_MO / PRD_PPBOM readers have no document-status filter — draft demand rows included
confirmed.field_or_branch: extra_filter (absent) on the three demand-side configs → parent header, finished-goods 订单数量, BOM rows
confirmed.paths_affected: Default live path (parent built from sales_orders[0] at mto_handler.py:698; finished-goods children from sales_by_key :636-659; BOM-first rows from production_bom :605) and cache (same configs drive sync)
confirmed.claim: A 状态A (draft/创建) sales order's qty appears as finished-goods 订单数量 and can become the parent header row; draft production orders/PPBOM rows likewise enter BOM demand. May be deliberate (show in-flight demand), but it contradicts the project's own stated rule (seed_data.py:174-178 「通常只查询有效单据：FDocumentStatus IN ('B','C','D')」) and is asymmetric with PUR_PurchaseOrder which excludes drafts — so demand and fulfillment columns mix different status 口径.
confirmed.impact: Only when an order is in draft/un-audited (status A) state while carrying an MTO number — e.g. a sales order temporarily 反审核'd for amendment. During that window the dashboard shows its qty as finished-goods 订单数量 and parent header (customer/delivery date) using unapproved values, while receipt/delivery columns exclude drafts, and a draft PUR order's qty silently degrades to the BOM need_qty fallback. For normal MTOs with approved orders (the overwhelming majority), output is identical with or without the filter. No wrong numbers on typical queries; this is a 口径-consistency gap visible only in amendment/draft windows.
confirmed.verifier_reasoning: Code facts fully verified by re-reading factory.py: exactly 5 FDocumentStatus filters (lines 344/363/382/418/436); PRODUCTION_ORDER_CONFIG (266-290), PRODUCTION_BOM_CONFIG (292-326), SALES_ORDER_CONFIG (462-493) — and also SUBCONTRACTING_ORDER_CONFIG (385-400), which the scanner missed — have none, and the fetch path (factory.py:144-175) adds nothing else. Default live path reachable: _fetch_live consumes these readers directly (mto_handler.py:597-605, parent at 698, finished goods at 636-659, BOM at 683); no downstream draft filtering exists. However, intent evidence is mixed: commit 827c496 deliberately scoped filters to 'receipt/delivery queries', and the project KB hard rule (seed_data.py:360, ontology.py:246-249) mandates the filter only for 入库单 receipt forms — all of which comply. The asymmetry the scanner cites is real but was introduced later by audit commit 11927dc adding the filter to PUR_PurchaseOrder only. So: real, undocumented 口径 inconsistency (demand columns in one table mix draft-inclusive and draft-exclusive sources), but it is partially semi-deliberate, has a plausible benign rationale (orders un-audited for amendment stay visible instead of making the whole MTO vanish), and manifests only with unusual data (draft order carrying an MTO). Confirmed at severity low — the scanner's own rating — not a default-path wrong-numbers bug. A fix would need a business decision (filter vs keep visibility), not a mechanical patch.

confirmed.verifier_reasoning: Confirmed by direct read: factory.py:386 form_id="SUB_SUBREQORDER" vs factory.py:536-537 docstring "(SUB_POORDER)" and CLAUDE.md:144 table row "SUB_POORDER | 委外订单". refuted.title: cached_material_picking.bill_no is written but never SELECTed by the cache reader (identity-only column) — verified harmless today, latent if a consumer ever re-dedups
refuted.lines: 253-262 (SELECT omits bill_no), 1539-1566 (_row_to_material_picking leaves model bill_no at default)
refuted.why_refuted: All cited code re-verified: sync_service.py:892-905 writes bill_no; cache_reader.py:253 SELECT omits it; _row_to_material_picking (1557-1566) leaves model bill_no at the '' default (models.py:114). But bill_no's purpose in this table is the write-time upsert identity key — explicitly documented in sync_service.py:878-882 ('bill_no is part of the logical identity ... Pattern 5; migration 018') — and the read path never needs it. Exhaustive consumer trace: get_material_picking has exactly one caller (mto_handler.py:434, inside _try_cache, reachable only via explicit ?source=cache which is slated for deletion), and its output dead-ends at line 442 (material_picks_07 is never used). The bill_no-keyed dedup the scanner flagged as the latent risk (_unique_document_nodes, mto_handler.py:307-327) is fed exclusively by live readers (line 278). The over-pick SQL (cache_reader.py:1124-1191) aggregates the raw table where bill_no rows remain physically distinct, so migration 018's fix is intact. The scanner itself states 'No current wrong output' — its claim requires a hypothetical future consumer on a deprecated path. Accurate code observation, but not a defect: no wrong output under any current code path or data shape. Pattern-1 requires the defaulted field to be CONSUMED downstream; here it is not.
